mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but it may require careful resource management and tuning for optimal performance.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface (CLI) to interact with the llm-d platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64

chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64

sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Download the latest CLI binary for Linux.
> Make the binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster using Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig to use the new cluster, allowing kubectl to interact with it.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch credentials for the created cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to verify they are ready.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm, deploying it to the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install the llm-d application in the llm-d namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d application from the cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, which may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstall the llm-d application.
> Delete the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your configurations and data. This step will upgrade the llm-d application to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current llm-d configurations.
> Upgrade the llm-d application.
> Verify that the llm-d pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that your kubeconfig is not correctly set up or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1' to refresh your kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image: Error response from daemon: pull access denied'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the image specified in the Helm chart is not accessible or does not exist.

**Fix:**
Check the image name in the Helm chart and ensure it is correct and accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on llm-d pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the application is failing to start due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n llm-d' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the llm-d application.

**Fix:**
Scale up the cluster or adjust the resource requests/limits in the Helm chart.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new inference scheduling algorithms and improved autoscaling capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy configuration options for resource limits.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your configuration files to remove deprecated options and adapt to the new scheduling algorithms.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' when running 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d', indicating that all llm-d pods are running successfully.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
